He urged Union ministers to highlight the opposition's stance as anti-women to the public. The bill did not secure the required majority in the Lok Sabha. The government remains committed to implementing women's reservation by the 2029 Lok Sabha elections.

articles.description: Opposition leaders have strongly criticized Prime Minister Narendra Modi's recent national address. They labeled it politically motivated and a breach of democratic conventions, especially given ongoing state elections. Leaders suggested the speech should be counted as a BJP campaign expense due to its partisan nature. They argued the address lacked substance and was an attempt to deflect from government failures.

articles.title: Samrat Choudhary signals continuity after taking over as Bihar CM; holds Janata Darbar, meets Nitish Kumar, orders anti-corruption push
articles.description: Bihar's new Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary is emphasizing continuity in governance. He has directed officials towards zero tolerance for corruption and efficient service delivery. Choudhary is also engaging in public outreach through a Janata Darbar. He is reaching out to senior leaders and plans cabinet expansion soon. Departmental reviews are also on the agenda.

articles.title: Jagan Mohan Reddy, Chandrababu Naidu criticise Opposition after Constitution Bill defeat; warn of impact on South’s representation and women’s quota
articles.description: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N Chandrababu Naidu and Opposition leader YS Jagan Mohan Reddy united in their criticism of the Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill's defeat. They expressed concern that blocking the bill would reduce southern states' representation and delay women's reservation. Both leaders believe this outcome denies justice and penalizes states that practice family planning.

articles.title: Ahead of Assembly polls, Punjab rolls out pilot of women cash support scheme in select constituencies; eligible women to receive Rs 1,000 monthly under AAP promise
articles.description: Punjab's Bhagwant Mann government has started a pilot of its Mukhymantri Mawan Dheeyan Satkar Yojana. This scheme offers monthly cash support to women. Registration began on April 15 for nine constituencies. Eligible women will receive ₹1,000 monthly, with SC women getting ₹1,500. Transfers begin in July. The government aims to register 90 lakh women.

articles.title: 'Women of this country will teach them a lesson,' BJP says after Constitution Amendment Bill fails; vows to continue push for 33% quota
articles.description: The BJP has strongly criticized the Congress party. This follows the failure of the Constitution (131st Amendment) Bill in the Lok Sabha. The bill aimed to provide 33% reservation for women in legislatures. BJP leaders accused the Congress of betraying women and blocking their political rights. The government expressed disappointment but vowed to continue the fight for women's representation.

They claim their concern is with the delimitation process, not the reservation itself. Leaders state the entire opposition will back the law's implementation. They accuse the government of using the issue for political gain and question the delay in enacting the legislation.

The party secured a majority, winning 24 out of 28 seats. This outcome marks a setback for the BJP's efforts to increase its influence in the region. The Tipra Motha Party's win signifies a strong mandate from the tribal population.

Raje dismissed it as fake, calling it the work of mischief makers. She reiterated support for PM Modi and said efforts on women’s representation are being widely welcomed.

The party challenged the PM to bring a bill in Parliament to implement women's reservation immediately within the existing Lok Sabha setup. Congress accused the government of deceitful attempts regarding women's reservation and delimitation proposals. The party highlighted its consistent stand on immediate implementation.

This move coincides with Anthropic's reported entry into web design, potentially competing with Figma's core products. Krieger's departure was confirmed in a filing, stating it was not due to any disagreements with Figma.

articles.title: NDA MPs wave banners in protest after women's reservation bill fails in Lok Sabha - Watch
articles.description: National Democratic Alliance's women MPs staged a protest on Parliament premises on Friday shortly after the Constitution (One Hundred and Thirty-First Amendment) Bill, 2026 failed to pass the Lok Sabha test.

articles.title: India not to host COP 33 in 2028, Government confirms
articles.description: Says move after review of its commitments for 2028; remains fully committed to meeting its responsibilities towards mitigating climate change

articles.title: TSMC expects its 2026 sales to grow more than 30%
articles.description: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. expects significant growth in 2026. Strong demand for artificial intelligence applications will drive sales up by more than 30 percent. The company is investing heavily in advanced chip manufacturing. This strategic move aims to solidify its leading position in the global market. TSMC's outlook reflects confidence in the burgeoning AI sector.

articles.title: India drops proposal to mandate national ID app Aadhaar on smartphones after pushback
articles.description: India's government has dropped a proposal to pre-install the Aadhaar app on smartphones. This decision follows opposition from smartphone makers like Apple and Samsung. They raised concerns about security, costs, and manufacturing. Privacy advocates also criticized Aadhaar due to past data leaks. The government previously withdrew a similar mandate for a telecom app.

articles.title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang warns against curbs on US chip sales to China
articles.description: Nvidia chief executive Jensen Huang has cautioned against US restrictions on advanced chip exports to China, warning that the curbs are accelerating Chinese capabilities and hurting American companies. The statements were made on a podcast hosted by Dwarkesh Patel on April 15.

articles.title: EU updates age-check app after vulnerabilities found
articles.description: The European Union is updating its new age verification app. This comes after independent developers identified security flaws. The app aims to protect children online. The European Commission stated that immediate steps are being taken to fix the issues. A new version is being developed. The app's code was made public for testing and improvement.

articles.title: India smartphone shipments fall to 6-year low in Q1 amid price hikes: Counterpoint
Parliament is debating reservation for women but it doesn’t have the infrastructure to receive them
Apple’s iPhone shipments in China surge 20% in Q1, data shows
Every time you use GPS, you rely on Einstein’s relativity laws — here’s how
Shashi Tharoor Column: Has justice in India become a luxury good?
articles.description: In this commentary, Shashi Tharoor addresses the deepening crisis within India’s judicial system crippling delays, unaffordable litigation, undertrial incarceration, and institutional inertia. As India aspires to become a Viksit Bharat, the piece argues that development rings hollow without timely, inclusive, and accessible justice for all.

articles.title: OpenAI expands Codex beyond coding with new AI capabilities
Anthropic talks to EU, including on its cyber security models, Commission says
articles.description: US-based ​artificial ​intelligence company Anthropic is ​currently in discussion with the European Commission on ‌its ⁠different ⁠models, including its ​cyber security ones, which are not ​yet available in the EU, the ​Commission said ⁠on Friday.

articles.title: From dream home to digital trap: How to stay safe from fake online property listings
What Centre calls judicial excess is actually the courts protecting those left behind
Walmart's Flipkart plans foray into India's ticketing market as live events boom, sources say
articles.description: Flipkart is set to enter India's booming entertainment market. The e-commerce giant plans to sell movie and concert tickets. This move pits Flipkart against established players like BookMyShow. The company is also piloting food delivery services. These expansions signal Flipkart's aggressive growth strategy in India.

articles.title: Column by Devdutt Pattanaik | Narada: the original provocateur
articles.description: Hindu mythology’s impish sage has been recast through the centuries, but never loses his identity

articles.title: Noida Protests: Why the Indian worker is reaching a breaking point
Goodbye tab hopping: Google Chrome’s new ‘AI Mode’ lets you browse and chat side-by-side
Tumour cells use a genetic trick to become drug-resistant
articles.description: As Paul Mischel of Stanford University describes in a paper in this week’s Cell, some cancer cells refuse to play along.

articles.title: ‘India is at the forefront of AI use’: Robert Kawalsky on Canva AI 2.0 launch
Krafton India launches third KIGI Cohort, sets stage for first incubator-born PC game
articles.description: KRAFTON India CEO Sean Hyunil Sohn said the programme reflects the broader momentum building in India’s game development scene, with more creators targeting international markets

articles.title: What one needs to build a tech unicorn: A dream, some employees and lots of AI hype
articles.description: AI promises to let startups do more with fewer people, but the plunge in unicorn headcounts may owe as much to VC hype as to real productivity gains. Is the world’s first solo unicorn not too far away? In some ways, it’s already happened.

articles.title: Life after Leonid Radvinsky: OnlyFans nears minority stake deal valuing it at $3 billion
articles.description: OnlyFans is close to selling a minority stake. The deal could value the adult content platform at over three billion dollars. San Francisco-based fund Architect Capital is in advanced discussions. An agreement might be reached next month. The sale follows the death of owner Leonid Radvinsky.

articles.title: Croma Akshay Tritiya sale 2026: QLED 4K tvs at Rs 50,990, ACs from Rs 26,590
BigBasket appoints ex-Google exec Arpit Jaiswal as chief growth officer
articles.description: BigBasket has appointed Arpit Jaiswal, a former executive of Google India, as its new chief growth officer. Jaiswal brings 15 years of experience from Google, where he led growth and monetisation for Google Pay. Additionally, Keshav Kumar has been elevated to the role of chief product & technology officer.

articles.title: Value investing: Has the Gulf war extended the life of an investment principle that Buffett made famous?
articles.description: Is value investing making a comeback? One may be tempted to think so, going by what we have seen in recent times, prompted by the so-called AI-scare trade. But with so much up in the air, investors can’t jump to conclusions yet.

articles.title: A third of our sales happen in non-metro cities, DailyObjects CEO says
articles.description: The company started with smartphone cases and then went deeper into charging solutions, power banks, cables, and bags

articles.title: EU spells out how Google must share data with rivals
articles.description: Under the EU's powerful DMA, the world's biggest tech companies must open up to competition to give consumers more options and limit abuses linked to market dominance

articles.title: White House to give U.S. agencies Anthropic Mythos access: Report
articles.description: Mythos has found “thousands” of ‌major vulnerabilities in operating systems, web browsers and ‌other ⁠software

articles.title: On seat guarantee, states need a proviso in Constitution, not just a promise in Parliament
EU in talks with Anthropic over risks of AI model Mythos
articles.description: Anthropic's announcement has encountered a mix of alarm and scepticism, with some pointing out the firm, one of several contenders in a fierce artificial intelligence race, stood to gain from the hype

articles.title: Ingenuity, the helicopter that flew over Mars
articles.description: Five years ago, on April 19, 2021, a helicopter called Ingenuity took off from the surface. In case you are wondering why this feat represents a Wright brothers’ moment in aviation history, it is because the surface in consideration was that of Mars! A.S.Ganesh retraces Ingenuity’s path as it became the first aircraft to achieve powered, controlled flight on another planet…

articles.title: India smartphone shipments fall to 6-year low in Q1 amid price hikes: Counterpoint Research
articles.description: India's smartphone shipments experienced a 3% year-on-year decline in Q1 2026, reaching a six-year low due to cost pressures and price hikes. Analysts predict further declines, with Vivo leading the market share. Google emerged as the fastest-growing premium brand, boosted by AI features.

articles.title: India’s rural models are shaping development diplomacy
articles.description: In recent years, a growing number of African governments have begun exploring the Indian SHG-based livelihoods framework

articles.title: ‘Political demonetisation — don't do it’: Shashi Tharoor tears into govt over Delimitation Bill
articles.description: Shashi Tharoor criticised the government for linking women's reservation to delimitation, calling it political demonetisation.&nbsp;

articles.title: OpenAI to spend more than $20 billion on Cerebras chips, receive equity stake: Report
articles.description: The commitments reported by ​The Information on ‌Thursday exceed OpenAI’s earlier reported agreement with the chipmaker

articles.title: Meet the man from Kozhikode who grew a bamboo forest
articles.description: How Damodaran Mukkam, a former landscaper, planted a bamboo forest on the banks of the Iruvazhanji river

articles.title: How community-led environmental initiatives are making a change in Ernakulam
articles.description: Ahead of Earth Day on April 22, we look at collectives in Ernakulam that are driving change through sustained, grassroots action

articles.title: U.S. cloud dominance a risk to European security: Report
articles.description: National security systems in 23 of the 28 countries studied "seem to rely on US tech", researchers found after looking at EU member states and Britain

articles.title: The origin stories of SPAM
articles.description: The months of April and May could well be celebrated as the birth months of spam — those notoriously undesired messages that clog our information highways. This is because the earliest known case of spam took place on May 3, 1978 and the first such case in the Web as we know it now took place on April 12, 1994. A.S.Ganesh turns off his spam filters to give you these two tales…

articles.title: Former Meta contractor Sama to lay off more than 1,000 workers in Kenya
articles.description: More than 1,000 workers in Kenya are facing layoffs after Meta ended its contract with the company Sama

articles.title: The discoverers of radio emissions from Jupiter
articles.description: The discovery of Jupiter’s “voice” – the first noise known to have come from a different planet – was announced to the world on April 5-6, 1955. At a meeting of the American Astronomical Society, Bernard F. Burke and Kenneth L. Franklin publicly disclosed their discovery of radio emissions from the largest planet of our solar system. A.S.Ganesh takes a look at both these men and their discovery…

articles.title: Does stress cause cancer? Experts say the answer is more complex than you think
articles.description: Limited mental bandwidth leads to delays in preventive care, impacting cancer outcomes. Experts emphasize managing stress rather than linking it directly to cancer, as chronic stress can foster unhealthy behaviors that increase overall health risks.

articles.title: New nasal spray shows promise fighting memory loss and brain inflammation
Wipro attrition falls to 13.8%, headcount inches up by 136
articles.description: Wipro reported a slight decrease in attrition to 13.8% and a significant slowdown in hiring, adding only 136 employees in Q4 FY26. This comes as the company's annual revenue declined 1.6% in constant currency terms, mirroring rival TCS's performance amidst geopolitical uncertainties and AI disruptions.

articles.title: Gucci-owner Kering aims to launch luxury Google glasses next year, CEO says
Stellantis, Microsoft sign five-year partnership for AI push
articles.description: Software and data-driven services have become central in the automotive companies’ ‌long-term strategies

articles.title: Earth Day 2026: A few initiatives from Thiruvananthapuram doing their bit for the environment
articles.description: Ahead of Earth Day, which falls on April 22, know a few people who have made a difference through community engagement and sustainability-focussed initiatives

articles.title: OpenAI launches AI model GPT-Rosalind for life sciences research
articles.description: The GPT-Rosalind is designed to support research across biochemistry, drug discovery and ‌translational medicine

articles.title: UK PM tells social media bosses to step up child online safety
articles.description: The government is considering restrictions on popular social media apps with ministers under pressure to introduce an Australia-style ban

articles.title: Uber to acquire additional 4.5% stake in Delivery Hero: FT
articles.description: Uber is set to acquire an additional 4.5% stake in rival Delivery Hero, purchasing approximately 270 million ​euros ($318.1 million) worth of shares at 20 euros each from Prosus. This move signifies a significant investment by Uber in its competitor, as reported by the Financial Times.

articles.title: Inside Andhra’s millet push that is turning women into entrepreneurs
articles.description: Across Andhra Pradesh, a government initiative is building women-led millet enterprises that link farms, kitchens and local markets into viable livelihoods

articles.title: How AI companies are quietly becoming the world’s cybersecurity gatekeepers
articles.description: Anthropic’s Project Glasswing looks noble on the surface. But it is essentially a cartel of the most powerful technology companies that gets to decide who can access the most capable cybersecurity tools ever built

articles.title: Perils of selective political reporting: the case of Tamil Nadu’s finances
articles.description: The rising prominence of social media warrants a closer look at the shifts in political reporting. An example is Union Minister Piyush Goyal’s recent claim on Tamil Nadu’s debt burden, which highlights the limits of citing debt without accounting for other factors

articles.title: Netflix shares dive as co-founder Reed Hastings steps away
articles.description: Netflix is pushing further into live sports, podcasts and games, executives said on an earnings call

articles.title: The institutionalised sluggishness of the legal system
articles.description: For a layman, the legal process feels like a black hole where time and money disappear without a clear horizon for resolution

articles.title: OpenAI launches AI model GPT-Rosalind for life sciences research
Starlink outage hit drone tests, exposing Pentagon's growing reliance on SpaceX
articles.description: US Navy tests of unmanned vessels were disrupted by Starlink outages, highlighting the military's growing reliance on Elon Musk's satellite network. Despite its crucial role in programs like drone operations and missile tracking, these incidents reveal potential vulnerabilities in national security capabilities due to dependence on a single provider.

articles.title: Starlink outage hit drone tests, exposing Pentagon’s growing reliance on SpaceX
articles.description: SpaceX’s Starlink broadband has been crucial to the Pentagon’s drone programme, providing connection to small unmanned maritime vessels that look like speedboats without seats
